stories.title: 2026 Training Camp Announced, Just SEVEN Practices Open To Public
stories.summary:
The Bills announced their training camp today in Rochester starting Wednesday July 29 and ending Friday August 7. The link below has the details but there are just seven practices open to the public with all practices at 8:30 am:



	https://www.buffalobills.com/news/buffalo-bills-announce-2026-training-camp-schedule-and-ticket-information



	 



	It is hard not to feel as though this may be the start of the end of Fisher & Rochester getting the Bills in a major way for camp. I have a few strong connections that the Bills even with extending the agreement may leave it early and they are looking to move back to Buffalo for the majority of camp sooner then later. Perhaps the Bills still do a long weekend get away at Fisher as the facilities are great for the team and it maintains a presence, but with the majority of teams now staying home for camp, a new stadium, and the Bills no longer needing the regionalization effort they succeeded with this is not a surprising outcome.

stories.title: 91 Buffalo Bills players in 91 days: defensive tackle Zane Durant
Would you take less salary to win a Super Bowl?
stories.summary:
This came up in the Knicks thread, I think it's a good question:



	 



	Why don't more QBs take a serious pay cut to have a better chance to win a Super Bowl? I would happily earn 40/million a year instead of 60/million if it upped my chances of getting a ring by 35-40%. Lifelong happiness of accomplishing the ultimate. 



	 



	This is probably why I'm not a billionaire (hahaha), but if you have 10 million dollars in the bank, I don't feel like money should be your prime motivating factor. Ultra-wealthy people and their chlidren are not happier than people who make enough to live comfortably, from what I see.

stories.title: Lessons from the Knicks
stories.summary:
As everybody is no doubt aware, one of New York State's *other* iconic teams, the NY Knickerbockers, had their most inspiring season and got the chip yesterday. They dominated like no other team in history in the playoffs. Crazy, because they had no player who seemed like a superstar, except Jalen Brunson. I think there are some lessons to be learned from them, and the coaching job Mike Brown did:



	 



	1) One superstar who can carry you in the clutch is huge--theirs was Brunson, ours is Josh, no problem there.



	 



	2) Regular season domination is highly overrated. The Knicks lost 9 of 11 in January. Better to play and learn together, and ebb and flow in preparation for the playoffs. The Knicks got huge contributions from unsung guys who felt trusted. Joe Brady, take note. 



	 



	3) A coach who is in tune with his players is major. Mike Brown is a player's coach, he gets them and does not work them into the ground at practice. (It's practice! Practice!) 



	 



	4) Lineup changes when players (even starters) aren't doing well can be major. The Spurs were a great team, but Brown switched it up a few times and it revitalized the Knicks. The Bills need to remember that when guys like Joey Bosa or Josh Palmer become liabilities late in the season.



	 



	5) A coaching change can do wonders. The Knicks got good under Tom Thibodeaux. Led them to 50 win seasons and the Eastern Conference finals, but got outcoached in the playoffs. The Knicks fired him and hired Mike Brown, a more intuitive guy, one year ago. The Knicks got over the hump his first year.



	 



	6) Your schemes should help your superstar, not burden them. Here is a quote from Brown, about Jalen Brunson: “It’s my job as a coach to fit whatever scheme we have on both sides of the floor to all of our players, and if you’re a great player, I’ve got to make a little bit more adjustments or I’ve got to give a little bit more than you do. And we finally got to a point where he was comfortable, I was comfortable. … The regular season is about finding your way so you can prepare for this time of the year, and there’s going to be a lot of ups and downs." Well, Brunson dominated the playoffs and scored 45 in the game to clinch the title.



	 



	Long story short, the Bills have had a number of problems similar to the Knicks, who figured it out. We gotta hope Brady will unleash the Bills, and especially Josh, like Brown unleashed the Knicks and Brunson. I truly believe this could be the start of a historic run of champ by both teams. LET'S GO!

stories.title: 2026 UFL thread.
stories.summary:
1. 60+ yard field goals = 4 points
	2.  One foot in bounds = catch
	3. Red zone penalties revert to half the distance to the goal. 
	4. The "Tush Push" is eliminated.
	5. No punting inside the opponent’s 50-yard line.
	6. Overtime: 3 alternating attempts per team from the 5 yard line.
	 
	7. Point aftertouchdown options:
	- 1 point – Kick from the 33-yard line
	- 2 points – Ball on the 2-yard line
	- 3 points – Ball on the 8-yard line



	 



	Season starts Friday, March 27.

stories.title: Former 1st round #12 pick Henry Ruggs: 3 to 10 yrs prison for drunk driving fatal crash
stories.summary:
https://www.espn.com/nfl/story/_/id/38162272/ex-raiders-wr-henry-ruggs-iii-sentenced-3-10-years-fatal-dui



	 



	
		Quote
	

	
		
			Ruggs, 24, had been under house arrest with alcohol and location electronic monitoring devices since pleading guilty in May to one count of DUI resulting in death and one count of misdemeanor vehicular manslaughter in the crash that killed Tina Tintor and her dog, Max. On Wednesday, Ruggs read from a statement directed at Tintor's family in which he apologized for his actions.
		

		
			"To the parents and family of Ms. Tintor, I sincerely apologize for the pain and suffering," Ruggs said.
		
	



	 



	Pretty sure Ms Tintor's family don't think 3 years is nearly enough punishment for burning her alive.
	
	Ruggs got what his attorneys asked for:



	
		Quote
	

	
		
			In court documents filed before the sentencing hearing, Ruggs' attorneys had asked the judge to keep the duration of their client's prison time to a range of three to 10 years. Chesnoff and Richard Schonfeld said Ruggs "wishes that he could turn back time and change the outcome of the tragic events of November 2, 2021."
		
	



	 



	I'm sure he does, what with being cut from the team and losing his career and all.  But he can't.
